title: corylopsis glaucescens
intro: Corylopsis glaucescens, also known as the yellow winter hazel, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Japan, characterized by its clusters of bright yellow flowers and dark green leaves. Traditionally, Corylopsis glaucescens has been used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in relieving sore throats and coughs. The health benefits of Corylopsis glaucescens include its possible ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Corylopsis glaucescens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety should be further studied.
